text: Created ticket Audit all shipped packages for overt bugs and robustness issues

Manager check-in on 2026-03-16 found `workspace-package-audit-worker` still in `ready/idle` with unresolved inbox backlog 1 (`workspace-package-audit-worker-msg-0001`) and no checkpoints. `manager_supervise` returned `continue (0.7)` and `manager_schedule` returned `resume — Worker has unresolved inbox backlog (1) and is a resume candidate`. I then triggered another `manager_write resume`, but the subprocess exited immediately again without acknowledging the assignment, resolving the inbox item, or writing any checkpoint/findings.

I corrected the worker to use the intended worker-side launch path and the preferred SDK runtime. Specifically, I ran `worker_resume` and `worker_launch` for `workspace-package-audit-worker` with `runtime: "sdk"`. Both launches reported `Execution: completed`, and durable state now records `lastRuntimeKind: sdk`. However, the worker still did not acknowledge `workspace-package-audit-worker-msg-0001`, did not write any checkpoint, and left unresolved inbox count at 1. This makes the audit ticket effectively blocked on a worker-runtime correctness issue rather than a manager-flow mistake. Follow-up bug ticket: `t-0041`.

Fixed the worker launch semantics in `packages/pi-workers` and verified them with targeted tests. I then launched `workspace-package-audit-worker` through the updated SDK runtime directly from the repo code path. The worker still made no durable progress, but it no longer lies about success: durable state now shows `failed/blocked` with summary `Launch reported completed but made no durable progress...`. Additional evidence from a direct SDK session probe: after `session.prompt(renderWorkerLaunchPrompt(worker))`, the session ends with an assistant message whose content array is empty (`lastRole: assistant`, `lastContent: []`, `messageCount: 2`). The audit worker is therefore blocked on a lower-level SDK/agent no-op condition rather than silent false success.

Package audit completed in the worker worktree. Concrete finding: full-suite `npm test` was flaky because `packages/pi-workers/__tests__/index.test.ts` and `packages/pi-plans/__tests__/store.test.ts` exceeded their default per-test timeout budgets under full-suite load while still passing in isolation. Landed a narrow robustness fix by raising those two test timeouts to `15_000` and `20_000` respectively, then verified with a clean repo-wide `npm test` pass. Created follow-up ticket `t-0043` to investigate and reduce the underlying latency rather than relying only on wider timeout budgets. Attached a package-by-package audit report covering all ten shipped packages and the exact verification sequence.

Worker workspace-package-audit-worker recorded checkpoint workspace-package-audit-worker-cp-0001: Completed the repo-wide shipped-package audit, fixed the concrete full-suite timeout robustness issue, and left durable evidence plus follow-up work.

metadata.summary: Audit assignment completed. The inbox is empty, durable evidence is attached to `t-0040`, the concrete full-suite timeout issue is fixed and verified, and the deeper latency question is tracked in `t-0043`.

text: Accepted and closed after review. Verified by the attached audit artifact, initial failing `npm test` reproduction, isolated `npx vitest run packages/pi-workers/__tests__/index.test.ts packages/pi-plans/__tests__/store.test.ts`, explicit per-package audit sweep across all ten shipped packages, and a final clean `npm test` run. Follow-up work remains tracked in t-0043.
